item.title: How Australia’s most iconic inland fish species has bounced back
item.description: Murray cod numbers have bounced back across much of Victoria and NSW, and that's good news for the whole Murray-Darling Basin ecosystem.

item.title: Liberals focus on 'lessons' for Victorian election after decisive Nepean by-election win
item.description: It took less than three hours from the start of vote counting for election analysts to call the result in the Liberals' favour, with a win for former local mayor Anthony Marsh.

item.title: Marta Kostyuk backflips as the Ukrainian defeats Russian Mirra Andreeva in Madrid Open final
item.description: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk backflips after beating Russian Mirra Andreeva in the Madrid Open women's final.

item.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder
item.description: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

item.title: Liberals claim victory for Jo Palmer, tight race shaping in Huon following Legislative Council elections
item.description: Voters in Rosevears have given Education Minister Jo Palmer a strong lead, while the race in the southern electorate of Huon is much closer with independent Dean Harriss fighting to retain his seat.

item.title: Why leucism is the explanation for this orange and black, red-tailed black cockatoo in WA's South West
item.description: Spotted again in WA's South West, a rare condition is the reason for the bird's distinctive orange colouring.

item.title: Fuel prices will not stop Mongolian family travelling across Australia and the world
item.description: While many Australians are thinking twice about big road trips due to fuel costs, the Sharavnyam family from Mongolia is determined to fulfil a dream to travel the world.

item.title: WA's delays on Working with Children Check, child safety changes criticised
item.description: Western Australia is the only state to not recognise Working with Children Check rejections from around the country, and has been "slow" to make other child safety law changes, expert says.

item.title: After Kumanjayi Little Baby's death, Coalition demands government re-engage on Indigenous debate
item.description: Nationals leader Matthew Canavan says after the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the government must "move on" from its defeat at the Voice referendum and re-engage on Indigenous policy debate.

item.title: Talking Pictures With Mike Bowers and Tom Ballard
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Socceroos attacker Mohamed Toure scores again for Norwich City to conclude three-month campaign
item.description: Mohamed Toure scores his 10th goal in just 12 matches for Norwich to keep him in red hot form ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Untold stories of women at Albany's whaling station inspire local artist
item.description: The overlooked contributions of women in the whaling industry are the subject of an exhibition in the Great Southern region of Western Australia.

item.title: Love story: When life changed in an instant, Lucy was Beau's rock
item.description: The goalposts moved dramatically on Beau and Lucy Vernon's life plans, but it brought them closer together.

item.title: Trundle ABBA Festival returns to transform farming community after break
item.description: For one day each year, the town of 335 people swaps tractors for tribute acts, grain trucks for glitter and everyday life for an all-out ABBA extravaganza.

item.title: State-owned land to be unlocked for oil storage and refineries under new Queensland government program
item.description: The state government has announced the Accelerating Fuel Infrastructure Program, which it says is intended to ease fuel costs and protect against future price shocks.

item.title: Marshall slams 'unacceptable' Tigers after horror second stanza
item.description: Benji Marshall said he could not recognise his Wests Tigers side after a humiliating second half saw Cronulla dominate at Shark Park, while there were also wins for Newcastle and Penrith on a bumper Sunday of NRL football.

item.title: Jamarra sparks Suns as teammate floors Oliver with bizarre face slap
item.description: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan makes his mark in the second half as tempers flare between Ben Long and Clayton Oliver in the fourth quarter.

item.title: Nepean by-election proof November state election will be tough contest for major parties
item.description: The Nepean by-election proves Victoria's election in November is likely to be tough for the two major parties, Richard Willingham writes.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese readies federal budget to favour gen Z and millennial voters
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is preparing to break an election commitment and overhaul property tax perks in this month's budget, to give younger Australians a "fair crack" at owning their own homes.

item.title: Man, 18, dies after car rolls over on Mitchell Freeway while travelling northbound
item.description: The man, who was the sole occupant of the vehicle, sustained critical injuries and died a short time later after being conveyed to Royal Perth Hospital.

item.title: What’s driving US market record highs despite oil crisis?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: This week in history quiz, May 4: Can you name the typo on the $50 note?
item.description: Take a trip back in time with this week's history quiz.

item.title: What can you use your personal leave for?
item.description: People employed on a permanent basis in Australia are entitled to 10 days' paid personal leave a year to help navigate illness, caring responsibilities, or family emergencies. But what are the different types of personal leave and when can you use them?

item.title: Australian police officers can be tracked due to a security flaw in tasers and body-worn cameras
item.description: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

item.title: The seven factors that will decide if the RBA raises interest rates on Tuesday
item.description: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

item.title: One Nation's rise could be more than a temporary protest, new polling says
item.description: A new poll asks Australians specifically about One Nation and the results show that rather than being a flash in the pan, the minor party's freight train may be a more lasting phenomenon.

item.title: The automation arms race will have huge consequences for workers
item.description: Artificial intelligence might be driving growth for data centre companies, but society is unprepared for the long-term risks posed to the rest of us.

item.title: Treasurer to bank tax windfall from Iran war in federal budget
item.description: The extra tax revenue generated by the Iran war will be saved in its entirety, as the government downplays major cost-of-living relief in next week's federal budget — though the treasurer warns those gains will be small.

item.title: Liveable Victoria launched to campaign against Labor's planning reforms
item.description: Residents' groups across Victoria have formed an alliance ahead of the state election, calling for the community and councils to be given a voice when it comes to approving high-density housing.

item.title: Revealed exhibition brings Aboriginal art from every corner of WA to the public
item.description: Revealed, Perth's long-running showcase of Aboriginal art from around Western Australia, allows budding artists to discover their creative side while strengthening their cultural bonds.

item.title: Tasmanian public housing tenants in unlivable and unhealthy conditions, and needing help from regulator
item.description: Despite unsafe and unusable bathrooms, cockroach infestations eating through wiring and constantly damp carpets, some Tasmanian public housing tenants are having to get the regulator to intervene in order to get solutions.

item.title: Essential services and cultural events scaled back in remote communities due to cost of fuel
item.description: Cultural obligations and essential services are being impacted by the cost of fuel in Australia's most remote communities.

item.title: Community fears West Gippsland hospital at Drouin will not meet 2028 target
item.description: Victoria's fast-growing West Gippsland region was promised a new hospital by 2028, but work has not started.

item.title: Future of National Trust of South Australia uncertain as ousted members demand parliamentary inquiry
item.description: The National Trust of South Australia admits it is cash-poor and will need to improve its finances, amid disagreements with its regionally-based volunteers.

item.title: Crinum mine former senior executives to face court over fatal roof collapse
item.description: Two former bosses are facing charges over a roof collapse at an underground mine in central Queensland in 2021 that killed a worker and seriously injured another.

item.title: How people power is helping to restore bus routes across Sydney
item.description: Communities fighting to have their local bus or ferry routes restored should campaign hard and "stick it out for the long term", according to these residents in Pyrmont.

item.title: Ex-Man United manager Alex Ferguson hospitalised after feeling unwell at Premier League match
item.description: Sir Alex Ferguson is sent to hospital after feeling unwell ahead of his former club's Premier League match against Liverpool.

item.title: E-bike sales surge 300 per cent in Darwin as more people ditch cars and costly fuel
item.description: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

item.title: Cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz reports being attacked as peace negotiations continue
item.description: A cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz says it has been attacked by multiple small craft, marking at least two dozen attacks in and around the strait since the Iran war began.

item.title: NAB profit drops as bank warns of Middle East uncertainty — as it happened
item.description: The ASX has slipped to its ninth loss in 10 sessions despite strong corporate earnings propelling the S&P 500 to a new record close. Look back at how the trading session unfolded on our business blog.

item.title: Albanese heralds 'deep friendship' with Japan following Takaichi meeting — as it happened
item.description: Australia and Japan sign agreements on critical minerals, energy security and defence cooperation following a meeting between Anthony Albanese and Takaichi Sanae.

item.title: Sam Kerr becomes Chelsea's all-time leading WSL scorer in 3-1 win over Leicester
item.description: Sam Kerr creates history in Chelsea's 3-1 win at Leicester, as the Blues secure a berth in next season's Champions League.

item.title: Coastal council pushes for levy on fossil fuel giants to fund disaster recovery
item.description: Councillors in a Victorian shire battling coastal erosion say fossil fuel giants should help pay for damage caused by climate-related environmental disasters.

item.title: Residents say removal of sperm whale at Bellambi put them at risk of shark attack
item.description: Swimmers and surfers say they were given no warning before a 25-tonne whale carcass was towed past more than a dozen South Coast beaches, attracting sharks in its wake.

item.title: As voting opens in the ABC Classic 100: Greatest of All Time, we explore how classical music is thriving
item.description: As Australians vote for the greatest classical music of all time, we look at who is listening, how classical music is evolving, and how it fills the world around us.

item.title: Parents of teen who died after sudden cardiac arrest want to bring heart screening to young athletes
item.description: Two Canberra parents rocked by the sudden death of their teenage son are trying to help him leave a life-saving legacy.

item.title: Pat Cummins endures horror IPL over as Sunrisers fall to Knight Riders in seven-wicket loss
item.description: Despite taking a wicket, Pat Cummins bowls an over he will prefer to forget, as Sunrisers Hyderabad's five-match winning streak comes to an end.

item.title: Agriculture sector sees 'silver lining' in global disruptions as interest in farming surges
item.description: The agriculture sector says an increase in attention has been a "silver lining" amid supply chain pressures.

item.title: Australian rugby sevens player Teagan Levi joins NRLW's Gold Coast Titans
item.description: The Gold Coast Titans are celebrating a massive signing as they continue preparations for the 2026 NRLW season.

item.title: Healing ceremonies across Northern Territory grieve death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl today with crowds in Alice Springs to pay tribute by donning pink, her favourite colour.

item.title: Uncertainty for Albany businesses as ATCO Australia moves customers to bottled gas
item.description: ATCO Australia tells 8,000 customers they will need to find new energy sources or switch to bottled gas as it prepares to decommission its Great Southern gas network in WA.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina wins Catalonia Open to claim first title as an Australian citizen
item.description: Fighting back from a set down, Daria Kasatkina wins the Catalonia Open final in Spain.

item.title: China accused of trying to target Port Pirie and Hobart smelters
item.description: The SA treasurer claims a foreign government is undermining the profitability of the struggling Port Pirie metal smelter.

item.title: Traffic chaos at Mt Field National Park with visitors lining up to see the turning of the fagus
item.description: Nature lovers who went to see the autumn colours of the fagus at Tasmania's Mt Field National Park on Saturday say there were no car spaces and nowhere to turn around.

item.title: Tim Roth battles Radha Mitchell in Australian action movie Seven Snipers
item.description: Shooting in snake-and-spider-infested grass on a Queensland farm, Seven Snipers spooked its stars.

item.title: Socceroos midfielder Aiden O'Neill injures ankle ahead of World Cup, as Harry Souttar returns
item.description: US-based Socceroos midfielder Aiden O'Neill suffers an ankle injury less than six weeks out from the start of the FIFA World Cup in North America.

item.title: Have your say: What would you like to see in the 2026 federal budget?
item.description: We want to know what issues matter to you ahead of the federal budget. Have your say.

item.title: Dolphins prop Daniel Saifiti retires from NRL due to ongoing shoulder problems
item.description: Daniel Saifiti's stellar career with the Dolphins, Newcastle, NSW, and Fiji comes to a premature end due to injury.

item.title: Four easy dishes with rice as the star
item.description: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

item.title: Jail then deportation for New Zealander who reversed over crowd outside Port Elliot pub in SA
item.description: A New Zealand national is jailed for six years after he admitted to reversing into a crowd of people at a South Australian pub.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos reveal timeline of Deine Mariner's leg injury that led to urgent surgery
item.description: Brisbane Broncos's chief medical officer releases the timeline of Deine Mariner's serious leg injury against the Roosters, leading to "limb-saving surgery" at Sydney's Royal Prince Alfred Hospital.

item.title: Shock after baby dies, another hospitalised after birth in Wagga Wagga homeless camp
item.description: A women's health advocate says the death of a baby during childbirth at a homeless camp on the Murrumbidgee River is a "terrible day" for the Wagga Wagga community.

item.title: Victorian court hears allegations Nicky Winmar 'bashed' woman's head against door
item.description: A Victorian court has heard allegations former AFL great Nicky Winmar "bashed" a woman's head against a wooden door with such force it cracked during an alleged assault in May 2025.

item.title: Alleged Anzac Day hecklers face court over 2025 Shrine of Remembrance booing
item.description: Witnesses have told a court they were "disgusted" when a group of men interrupted last year's Anzac Day Dawn Service in Melbourne.

item.title: SA public servants asked to remove word 'harmful' from communications about SA algal bloom, FOI shows
item.description: Emails released under FOI laws and obtained by the SA Liberal Party show government workers were removing the word "harmful" from communications about the algal bloom in SA waters, with one team leader labelling it "bizarre".

item.title: Authorities issue warning for bushfire at Herdsman Lake in suburban Perth
item.description: Authorities issue a warning for possible ember flare-ups as an unseasonal bushfire burns at Herdsman Lake in suburban Perth.

item.title: Man accused of murdering parents and brother denied bail, referred for psychiatric assessment
item.description: Jacky Amazing Feng, 32, faced court for the first time on Monday, following the alleged murder of three family members and attempted murder of another.

item.title: Australia and Japan sign agreements on energy, defence and critical minerals
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Japanese counterpart Takaichi Sanae agreed to bolster cooperation on energy, defence and critical minerals, while warning against economic coercion.

item.title: AFL commentary icon Dennis Cometti farewelled at state memorial service in Perth
item.description: A host of high-profile Australians gather at Perth Stadium to pay tribute to one of the nation's most well-known sports broadcasters, Dennis Cometti.

item.title: AFL umpire Nick Foot's betting agency role over after tribunal case
item.description: Betting agency Sportsbet announces it will no longer feature current sports administrators or officials in their programming "following feedback".

item.title: Treasurer gives reality check on Anthony Albanese's hopes for universal child care legacy
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has previously committed to delivering universal child care, but Treasurer Jim Chalmers has highlighted concerns about the cost.

item.title: Iran says it turned back US warship from Strait of Hormuz, US denies missile strike
item.description: The US military has denied that any of its Navy ships have been hit in the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian state media reported that a US frigate was targeted by two missiles.

item.title: Brisbane Lions player Koby Evans under investigation for alleged homophobic slur in VFL
item.description: The AFL faces another homophobic slur case, with a Brisbane Lions player alleged to have used a slur in a VFL game against Coburg.

item.title: Super Netball round-up: A 'wishy washy' approach to Pride matches dulls the message
item.description: Several Super Netball clubs will host their own Pride matches this season, but without an official round to anchor them all a former player and member of the LGBTIQA+ community argues the message may be lost.

item.title: Electric vehicle tax discount to be wound back
item.description: A popular tax discount meaning people leasing electric vehicles did not have to pay Fringe Benefits Tax will be wound back — although in the long term, more EV owners will be able to pay a reduced tax rate.

item.title: Cruise ship with suspected hantavirus outbreak banned from docking in Cape Verde
item.description: Three people have died and three people are ill after a suspected outbreak of the rodent-borne virus in the Atlantic Ocean.

item.title: Hegseth says ceasefire 'is not over', with US 'not looking for fight'
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the ceasefire with Iran is still in effect, despite the US and Iran launching attacks in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday. Read how developments on Tuesday April 5 unfolded.

item.title: World order could be 'rebuilt out of Europe', Carney says
item.description: The Canadian prime minister tells European leaders the world does not need to submit to a "more transactional and insular world".

item.title: Met Gala quiz: Who Am I? Guess who wore these outfits to fashion's best party
item.description: From Ariana to Zendaya, we're testing your knowledge of some of the most exceptional and extreme fashion on show at recent Met Galas.

item.title: Labor locks in tax reform trio: capital gains, negative gearing and trusts
item.description: The government is still finalising the details of its budget, but several sources say three tax policies that featured in Labor's 2019 election campaign will be revived and rethought.

item.title: The NDIS rorts playbook that's popping up around Australia
item.description: Insiders and participants say the NDIS is being targeted in similar ways around the country, including billing for services not provided, overcharging and in some cases "kidnapping" of vulnerable clients in order to park them in expensive supported accommodation and drain their plans.

item.title: Dialysis units across Australia and New Zealand forced to ration lifesaving care
item.description: Dialysis units across Australia and New Zealand are being forced to ration lifesaving care for patients with kidney failure, according to a major new report which has warned of a "significant threat" to patient safety.

item.title: RBA's decision on interest rates a choice between two evils: recession or inflation
item.description: If there's any certainty about the RBA's interest rate verdict, it's that it will be a split decision.

item.title: Teen with disability sues Australian government over treatment during Vanuatu quake
item.description: A teenager with multiple disabilities stranded for days in Vanuatu's capital city after a deadly 2024 earthquake is taking the Australian government to the federal court for disability discrimination.

item.title: Hung Vuong Stadium construction begins as part of Vietnam transformation plan
item.description: The country announced more than 500 infrastructure projects last year alone, totalling $280 billion, as it pushes ahead with an ambitious plan to revolutionise its economy.

item.title: Gavin Preston murder theory 'completely implausible', defence says
item.description: Lawyers for two men accused of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston tell a jury their clients did not have the skills required to carry out a well-planned execution.

item.title: What Jarome Luai's shock NRL move could mean for Australian and New Zealand rugby union
item.description: The NRL's expansion has ramifications for more than the existing 17 clubs, with Australian and New Zealand rugby talent also in the crosshairs.

item.title: Early voting opens in Stafford by-election with three main issues to sway votes in Queensland state seat
item.description: Residents in the north Brisbane seat of Stafford are set to cast their votes in a by-election dominated by cost-of-living concerns.

item.title: Two rescuers among three dead in boating accident on NSW coast
item.description: Marine Rescue NSW responds to a yacht in distress but its vessel rolls in heavy conditions.

item.title: Push for free school lunch program to be adopted nationally
item.description: Tasmania is "leading the way" with its largely state government-funded school lunch program. Now advocates are calling for a national rollout of the scheme, but warn parents may need to chip in.

item.title: Supermarkets urged to absorb more price increases from Iran conflict
item.description: As the price of food and agricultural production goes up during the Iran conflict, supermarkets are being urged to absorb more of the cost.

item.title: Anglican bishop ordered to resign over relationship with priest appealing decision
item.description: One of South Australia's most senior Anglicans is stripped of his position over an undisclosed relationship with a priest he ordained, in a case described by the church as "a gross ethical failure".

item.title: $150 million 'chocolate experience' proposal approved by Glenorchy council
item.description: Glenorchy City Council has approved plans for a $150 million "chocolate experience" to be built at the Cadbury factory site in Claremont in Hobart's northern suburbs.

item.title: Jefferson Lewis excused from first court appearance since 5yo Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged murder
item.description: The man accused of murdering five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby has been excused from appearing in court in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Pharmacists turn patients away for home medicine reviews due to funding caps
item.description: Pharmacists say government funding caps limiting the number of patients they can see in their homes for medication reviews means many are missing out on life-changing help.

item.title: WA government preparing to abandon its emissions reduction targets, documents reveal
item.description: The WA government is preparing to walk away from its own greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets and dramatically reshape how the state addresses climate change, the ABC can reveal.

item.title: PM brushes aside questions over broken promise on negative gearing — as it happened
item.description: Anthony Albanese has brushed aside questions on if the government has broken a promise to not touch negative gearing in the upcoming budget. Take a look back at the day's coverage below.

item.title: High Court to consider who owns rights to abattoir footage captured by activists' secret cameras
item.description: A goat abattoir, a group of animal rights activists and the copyright act will be the focus today in a High Court challenge over who owns the rights to footage captured by secret cameras.

item.title: Separated couples forced to keep living together amid housing crisis
item.description: Experts say more separated couples are continuing to stay under the same roof because housing is too expensive or scarce.

item.title: French Open prize money raise criticised by Jannik Sinner and Aryna Sabalenka as not being enough
item.description: Top players, including world number ones on the men's and women's tour, criticise the increased French Open prize money pool as not being enough amidst other unresolved welfare demands.

item.title: RBA governor warns Australians to brace for inflation to get worse despite rate hikes — as it happened
item.description: The RBA delivers another rate rise while the local share market closes lower amid escalating tension in the Middle East. Look back on how the day unfolded with our specialist business reporters on our blog.

item.title: Justin Baldoni and Blake Lively settle legal battle over It Ends With Us scandal
item.description: The pair settle their civil case two weeks before a trial had been set to begin in New York.

item.title: Met Gala 2026 red carpet: Beyoncé, Rihanna, Heidi Klum, Bad Bunny and Kylie Jenner
item.description: A quick glance at the outfits from one of the fashion world's biggest events of the year.

item.title: La Di Da nightclub targeted by Melbourne arsonists as industry figures fear escalation of booze wars
item.description: La Di Da nightclub is the latest licensed venue to be targeted by arsonists in Melbourne, as industry representatives fear an escalation of black market alcohol wars.

item.title: Remote working encouraged at Surrey County Cricket Club to boost attendance at The Oval
item.description: Working from home has become a mainstay of many economies since the COVID-19 pandemic. Surrey County Cricket Club figured that if people can work from home, maybe they can work from The Oval, too.

item.title: Family of boy with extremely rare medical condition seeks funding for life-changing treatment
item.description: Michele Dichiera is one of 20 children in the world with giant cell angioblastoma, but a life changing treatment could cost his family $9,500 per month.

item.title: Formula 1's electrical revolution is losing its spark and V8 power could return
item.description: As drivers continue to raise objections to Formula 1's new electrical power era, FIA president Mohammed Ben Sulayem has suggested a return to V8 power.

item.title: Wollongong priest stands trial accused of sexually assaulting student
item.description: A priest at two Wollongong parishes has been accused of sexually assaulting an international university student in 2019.

item.title: Clare Reilly becomes first person to complete 1,000km Munda Biddi trail in a wheelchair
item.description: The multiple sclerosis advocate undertook the 1,067-kilometre challenge to raise money for life-changing research.

item.title: These dads are putting down the tools and picking up a hair brush
item.description: The viral social media trend encouraging dads to learn how to style their daughter's hair is challenging gender roles in country Australia.

item.title: Hugh Jackman on his new murder mystery film Sheep Detectives
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ex-AFL player Aiden O'Driscoll opens up on brain trauma hell and compensation fight
item.description: Head trauma ended Aiden O'Driscoll's promising AFL career before he could play his first game. Now, like many former footballers affected by brain injuries, he faces an uncertain future as he fights for compensation.

item.title: Monarto Safari Park hyena gets gold-coloured crown in dental treatment
item.description: One of Monarto Safari Park's hyenas is flashing its new golden smile after a dental procedure to fix its deformed teeth and restore biting abilities.

item.title: Embracing being 'wasian' with a little help from Laufey
item.description: Being biracial or "wasian" (that is, both white and Asian) is a unique experience. There are lots of things I share with both Asian and white people, but there are experiences novel to being wasian.

item.title: Removal of no-grounds evictions in WA could signal more policy backdowns
item.description: The WA government's decision to axe no-grounds evictions could signal more policy backdowns as politicians adjust to a changing political landscape, writes state political reporter Keane Bourke.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Hawthorn ready to face Fremantle amid challenge from star ruckman Luke Jackson
item.description: Hawthorn faces a major test on Thursday night against Fremantle in Perth as the Dockers sit on a seven-game winning streak.

item.title: PM leaves door open to tax offset for workers as Coalition raises inflation concerns
item.description: A one-off tax break for working Australians remains a live option for cost-of-living relief as Labor finalises plans for next week's federal budget.

item.title: Australian author Craig Silvey pleads guilty to possessing child exploitation material
item.description: Author Craig Silvey, best known for his books Jasper Jones and Runt, pleads guilty to distributing and possessing child exploitation material.

item.title: Victorian government spruiks first surplus in seven years in 2026 budget
item.description: The state government has failed to meaningfully tackle soaring debt, instead continuing to focus on cost-of-living programs and service delivery less than 200 days before voters head to the polls.

item.title: Search suspended for missing Australian hiker Denise Ann Williams in Canada
item.description: The search for a missing Australian woman on a Canadian island off the country's east coast has been suspended.

item.title: Who are the winners and losers of Victoria's 2026 state budget?
item.description: Despite rising debt, the Victorian government has found money for hip-pocket measures and a justice system overhaul. These are some of the winners and losers from the 2026-27 budget.

item.title: Transport for NSW shortlists possible fixes to a failed section of the Great Western Highway
item.description: Australian and international engineering firms are being shortlisted as they propose solutions to the failed section of the Great Western Highway.

item.title: Calls for homelessness solutions intensify after death of baby in makeshift camp
item.description: Community leaders in Wagga Wagga are demanding immediate action after a baby died and another was hospitalised during childbirth at a riverside camp.

item.title: Sex offender Frankie Bailey banned from providing health services after counselling child
item.description: A former school principal who served jail time for child sex offences has allegedly posed as a healthcare professional to "come into contact with children".

item.title: Sydney cage fighter says he has no memory of trying to dispose of friend's body, murder trial hears
item.description: A Sydney cage fighter on trial for murdering his best friend during a backyard sparring session has been accused by prosecutors of making up evidence.

item.title: Mortgage repayment calculator: How much will the RBA's latest rate hike cost you?
item.description: Tell us your loan size, term and interest rate, and our mortgage repayment calculator will work out how much extra you may have to pay on your loan.

item.title: Self-proclaimed investigative journalist granted bail over 'satantic' child abuse material charges
item.description: A self-described journalist has been granted bail after telling police he accessed "satanic" child abuse files to "build a bigger case" up against the abuse ring detectives were investigating.

item.title: Reserve Bank lifts interest rates by another 0.25pc to 4.35pc
item.description: The RBA lifts rates by 0.25 basis points to 4.35 per cent, which fully unwinds last year's rate cuts. But governor Michele Bullock says the rate rises will not stop higher inflation heading Australia's way from the Iran war.

item.title: A drive-through of multiple speed limit changes on Beach Road south of Adelaide
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Firefighter threatened to 'skin' Jewish paramedic, antisemitism royal commission told
item.description: Paramedic Joshua Gomperts tells the royal commission into antisemitism how a firefighter pulled a hunting knife on him during a music festival in regional Victoria.

item.title: Major banks lift interest rates after Reserve Bank announces cash rate rise for third consecutive time
item.description: Australia's big four banks have all lifted their interest rates for customers following the RBA's decision to increase the national cash rate to 4.35 per cent, a third consecutive hike.

item.title: LIV Golf crisis makes future unclear for Australians Cameron Smith, Elvis Smylie
item.description: Australia's LIV golfers including Cameron Smith are being offered support by the sport's hierarchy amid a funding crisis, as a golf executive says the 2022 British Open champion is "too young to retire".

item.title: WA government defends decision to abandon emissions reduction targets
item.description: The WA government concedes the state's greenhouse gas emissions may continue to rise, after the ABC revealed the government is abandoning plans to implement interim emissions reduction targets.

item.title: The RBA is risking a recession by raising interest rates but feels there's nothing else it can do
item.description: Monetary policy trade-offs are getting worse. The Reserve Bank of Australia is confronting rising inflation and downgraded growth forecasts. And there's nothing it can really do about it.

item.title: Tobacco giant's secret appearance at inquiry raises questions, health minister says
item.description: The federal health minister and health groups are raising concerns about a secret appearance of cigarette giant Philip Morris at a parliamentary inquiry examining black market tobacco.

item.title: Nicky Winmar's barrister tells Magistrates' court AFL great was defending himself
item.description: The barrister for Nicky Winmar has told a Victorian court the former AFL great was defending himself on the night he allegedly assaulted a woman.

item.title: AFL fines Carlton $75,000 in wake of Elijah Hollands's mental health episode against Collingwood
item.description: Three weeks on from Elijah Hollands's mental health episode at the MCG, the AFL fines Carlton $75,000 for its handling of the player and issues around the incident.

item.title: Calls for CIT CEO Margot McNeill to resign following damning report released by ACT Legislative Assembly
item.description: Canberra Insititute of Technology's chief executive Margot McNeill is under pressure to resign, after the ACT Legislative Assembly released a damning report which found she misled the institute's board.

item.title: Puppy torturing couple heckled by locals after bail renewed
item.description: Nathan James Dekkers and Isabel Maree Busher will remain on bail, with sentencing for their involvement in the mistreatment and death of an 11-week-old puppy adjourned until October.

item.title: Surfers Paradise Esplanade closed permanently after tense council vote
item.description: The Gold Coast council has voted to permanently close part of the Surfers Paradise Esplanade despite division over traffic and business impacts.

item.title: Raising interest rates won't lower petrol prices. Here's why the RBA did it anyway
item.description: Sensible Emily untangles the latest interest rate decision and gives you tips to help you save money.

item.title: Court hears Victorian taxi operator reflect on 'tragic' wheelchair deaths after admission passengers put at risk
item.description: Echuca Moama Taxi Group put wheelchair passengers at risk of death or serious injury by failing to ensure passengers were properly secured, a Victorian court has heard.

item.title: Complaints about work placements grow from Notre Dame nursing students and staff
item.description: Numerous nursing students at Perth's Notre Dame University say issues with work placement have left them wondering if they will be able to graduate on time.

item.title: South Sydney star Latrell Mitchell breaks his media ban to discuss form, PNG offers
item.description: The NRL's strongest voice, Latrell Mitchell, breaks a lengthy league media ban to discuss his form, his positional switch and the potential of a move to PNG.

item.title: Concerns raised before Aboriginal teen's death in state care, inquest hears
item.description: A 17-year-old Aboriginal boy in state care was staying in a semi-independent living facility at the time of his sudden death, despite a witness raising concerns about his lack of supervision, an inquest has heard. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander readers are advised that this article contains the name of an Indigenous person who has died, with the permission of his family.

item.title: Vietnam veteran John O'Halloran given Medal for Gallantry in Perth
item.description: Vietnam veteran John O'Halloran is awarded Australia's third-highest military honour for leading a fixed bayonet charge on "one of Australia's worst days in South Vietnam".

item.title: Blast at fireworks factory in China's Hunan province kills at least 26 people, state media says
item.description: Authorities evacuated nearby residents and deployed rescue robots after the deadly explosion at the fireworks factory in Hunan province.

item.title: Melbourne club operators in fear of threatening texts, kidnappings and arson attacks
item.description: Melbourne is in the grip of a crime wave. But this time it's different and pub and club owners say they are scared as 'The Cartel' issues threats they do not understand.

item.title: Sydney childcare worker granted bail over alleged assault of young children
item.description: Jayendra Shahi was charged with allegedly assaulting two girls at a childcare centre in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: Inquest begins into 1998 disappearance of teenager Rebecca Richardson
item.description: Rebecca Richardson was a beloved teenager who went missing from Gin Gin in 1998. Her body has never been found and her family is desperate for answers.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Pubs and clubs on high alert after firebombings and threats of violence
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: 'Deeply offensive' video by comedian Alex 'Shooter' Williamson about Kumanjayi Little Baby condemned
item.description: South Australia's Commissioner for Aboriginal Children and Young People speaks out against a "deeply offensive" social media video posted by comedian Alex "Shooter" Williamson in the wake of the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Former AFL player's fight for compensation
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Human-to-human transmission suspected in deadly cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization says no rats have been found on board the cruise vessel where the outbreak has taken hold, with three people having died of confirmed or suspected infections of the rodent-borne virus.

item.title: Albanese government axes funding for $45 billion Inland Rail project linking Melbourne to Brisbane
item.description: The federal government has halted plans for the Melbourne to Brisbane Inland Rail, amid concerns the project could cost more than $45 billion to complete.

item.title: Singapore approves caning for school bullies under new anti-bullying guidelines
item.description: The move is part of Singapore's new anti-bullying measures introduced by the Ministry of Education.

item.title: Romania's pro-EU government collapses after no-confidence vote
item.description: The country's currency falls to a record low against the euro as concerns mount about the state of its finances.

item.title: UK police probe arson at ex-synagogue as PM decries antisemitism 'crisis'
item.description: The blaze is the latest in a spate of incidents that appear to have targeted the UK's Jewish community.

item.title: Travel quiz, May 6: Fancy yourself a jetsetter? These questions will test you
item.description: Which country does the alcoholic spirit cachaça hail from? And how much do you know about Australian towns with Big Koala statues?

item.title: How David Gee pulled off one of the most ambitious coin heists in Australian history
item.description: David Gee pulled off one of the most baroque and ambitious coin heists in Australian history, nicking precious coins from the special collections of the nation's oldest library without anybody noticing.

item.title: 'Australia's first attractive comedian' Elouise Eftos looks for love in Aphrodite
item.description: Last year, Elouise Eftos became the first Australian woman to land a best newcomer nod at Edinburgh Fringe. Now, she's got a new show about never falling in love.

item.title: More than 8 million people rely on income support, driven by a growing number of mental health claims
item.description: The number of people accessing income support payments has risen by 2 million in the past decade, with more Australians unable to work due to deteriorating mental and physical health.

item.title: Interest rate rises push employed people and young families into mortgage stress
item.description: Almost 14,000 Australians rang the National Debt Helpline last month, with most callers saying they are struggling to pay their home loans and are facing mortgage stress, according to new data.

item.title: Businesses need to raise prices or put Australians out of work
item.description: Australia had an inflation problem before the Iran war, but it's turned sinister. We've become hostage to oil prices, and as the governor of the Reserve Bank puts it: "We are all feeling poorer".

item.title: Farrer by-election could remake conservative politics in Australia
item.description: The first electoral test for both new coalition party leaders, Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan, sees the opposition at serious risk of losing Farrer for the first time since the seat was created in 1949.

item.title: Business as usual for Victoria's budget as inflation and interest rise before the state election
item.description: The most important number in the Victorian budget isn't listed in its pages. It's the 206 days until the polls, with the Labor government seeking an unprecedented fourth term.

item.title: Last Allan government budget before election charts future of increased revenue and debt
item.description: The latest figures released by the government show where its spending is going, and where the money to pay for it all is coming from.

item.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia's 'utter cynicism' as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire
item.description: The Ukrainian president called one attack in Poltava "especially vile", accusing Russia of launching a second missile while emergency rescuers were at the scene.

item.title: Charges dismissed against Salamanca Whisky Bar for allegedly selling 'fake' Sullivans Cove whisky
item.description: The Salamanca Whisky Bar was accused of selling fake versions of the award-wining Sullivans Cove whisky. But eight years after the saga began, a court in Hobart has dismissed the charges.

item.title: Phone books are being delivered around Australia but does anyone still use them?
item.description: Once a must-have household item, are phone books destined to go the way of the answering machine and rotary dial phone?

item.title: Live: Trump says plan to move ships through Hormuz to pause for possible peace deal
item.description: US President Donald Trump says Project Freedom, aimed at opening the Strait of Hormuz, will pause to try to get a peace deal signed with Iran. Follow live.

item.title: Small Australian designers giving up on fighting fast-fashion giant Shein over design duplicates
item.description: Small Australian fashion designers say it is too time-consuming to continue to ask Shein to take down copies of their designs.

item.title: Man charged with manslaughter, drug driving and stealing ute in Woodford crash
item.description: Police charge a 31-year-old Brisbane man with manslaughter following a fatal crash allegedly involving a stolen utility at Woodford.

item.title: Calls to commemorate K'gari's unknown war heroes with permanent tribute
item.description: Residents call for the creation of a permanent memorial for a secret military school on K'gari (Fraser Island) that trained elite soldiers during World War II.

item.title: EV tax discount removed two days' worth of carbon emissions
item.description: Two days' worth of carbon emissions were removed thanks to the $2 billion electric vehicle tax discount. But its supporters say that put 64,000 more EVs on the road, jump-starting the struggling industry.

item.title: Families could save up to $50 a week growing their own fruit and vegetables
item.description: With grocery prices continuing to bite, more Australians are discovering that home gardening isn't just good for wellbeing — it can also make real financial sense.

item.title: New Hilton hotel announced for Adelaide adds up to 15 under construction
item.description: The latest major hotel project announced for Adelaide adds to a looming accommodation construction boom in the CBD.

item.title: How do negative gearing, capital gains tax and trusts work?
item.description: The government is poised to make changes to negative gearing, the capital gains tax discount, and the tax treatment of trusts in next week's federal budget. So how do they work, and what could the changes look like?

item.title: Call for action on cold and flu products containing herb linked to anaphylaxis
item.description: Andrographis paniculata is a common ingredient in cold and flu supplements, but it may not be as low-risk as first thought.

item.title: Calls to protect culturally and linguistically diverse communities from 'anxiety-driven' NDIS cuts
item.description: Nidhi Shekaran is one of thousands of NDIS participants waiting to learn if their services will be impacted as part of sweeping government reforms to the scheme.

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family say they feel let down after missing her alleged murderer's court hearing
item.description: The family of a five-year-old girl who died after going missing from an Alice Springs town camp last month say they feel let down by the system after missing her alleged killer's first court date.

item.title: Coorparoo Bowls Club angry at Bowls Queensland claims it 'failed' to renew its lease
item.description: Coorparoo Bowls Club's committee says a paperwork blunder allowed Bowls Queensland to evict them from the land so they could sell it to developers.

item.title: Live: 'ISIS-brides' families plan to travel to Australia from Syria, government officially confirms
item.description: The federal government has confirmed 13 women and children, linked to Islamic State in Syria, have made plans to return to Australia. Follow live.

item.title: Talia Gibson recovers from dreadful second set to beat Martina Trevisan in Rome
item.description: Talia Gibson has made a fighting start on her debut on the clay of Rome, the Australian number two beating former world top-20 player Martina Trevisan to progress.

item.title: Fuel prices rising in the US as costs stabilise in Australia
item.description: Fuel prices in Australia have started to moderate just as costs have reached an almost four-year high in the United States.

item.title: Project Freedom has been paused, so will it lead to a diplomatic breakthrough or reignite war with Iran?
item.description: Iran had branded Project Freedom as "Project Deadlock" and now US president Donald Trump has placed the military's support mission in the Strait of Hormuz on hold.

item.title: Live: ASX rises following gains on Wall St
item.description: The ASX opens higher following fresh records on two of Wall Street's major stock exchanges. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Ballina boating tragedy witness describes frantic rescue efforts
item.description: Darragh Hunt was fishing at Ballina when the incident that claimed three lives unfolded before his eyes.

item.title: Arsenal reach Champions League final by beating Atletico Madrid to close in on historic double
item.description: Arsenal reach the Champions League final for the second time in their history with a 1-0 home win over Atletico Madrid sealing a 2-1 aggregate victory in the semi-final.

item.title: Inside Fontainebleau, the heritage-listed former home of Frederick McCubbin at Mount Macedon
item.description: The resident trying to take adverse possession of artist Frederick McCubbin's historic former home says he wants to bring in the "right people" to fix it up.

item.title: Arson squad investigates Chery car dealership fire in Port Melbourne
item.description: Two men are believed to have jumped a fence, smashed glass windows and set fire to a vehicle inside a Port Melbourne car dealership before fleeing, according to police.

item.title: Jon Rahm pays fine to return to DP World Tour as LIV Golf doubt grows
item.description: Jon Rahm will be eligible for Europe in next year's Ryder Cup after agreeing to pay fines and play events on the DP World Tour this season, a move that also triggered speculation about his future with LIV Golf beyond 2026.

item.title: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni issues warning amid circulation of deepfake photos
item.description: Giorgia Meloni shares an AI-generated photo of her posing on a bed and says her political opponents have been using such images to try to undermine her.

item.title: What age should a child start swimming lessons?
item.description: Swimming ability is declining in Australia, and experts say knowing the right age to enrol your children in lessons — as well as what to do if you can't access them — is key to improving water safety.

item.title: Tasmania's 30th governor announced as Caroline Wells, chair of Royal Flying Doctor Service Tasmania
item.description: Caroline Wells will become the 30th Governor of Tasmania after receiving formal approval from King Charles III, replacing Barbara Baker AC.

item.title: Sport Climbing Australia to introduce inclusive category for all genders, following IOC trans ban
item.description: For Phoebe and Luce, joining an LGBTQI+ social climbing group was transformative. But as a trans woman and non-binary person, they have faced barriers to participating in elite competition and are calling for change.

item.title: Iran's mosquito fleet a 'weapon of mass disruption' in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's so-called mosquito fleet of small, speedy attack boats has become an increasingly aggressive nuisance for the US Navy, as it attempts to break the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Publican stands trial accused of setting fire to pub for financial gain
item.description: A former publican has been accused of increasing the pub's contents insurance premium before deliberately setting it on fire in 2022.
